heading: Reports to Congress
label: 2673 Reports to Congress
text: The head of each federal agency shall report annually to Congress all claims paid by it under , stating the name of each claimant, the amount claimed, the amount awarded, and a brief description of the claim.section 2672 of this title
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 922 (, ).Aug. 2, 1946, ch. 753, § 40460 Stat. 843Changes were made in phraseology.

Repeal, , , repealed the requirement that an annual report to Congress be made of the administrative adjustment of tort claims of $2,500 or less, stating the name of each claimant, the amount claimed, the amount awarded, and a brief description of the claim.Pub. L. 89–348, § 1(1)79 Stat. 1310Nov. 8, 1965
